title: Machining Strategy Time Optimizer
description: Calculates optimal machining cycle time and identifies hidden losses using Lean, Six Sigma, and ISO 9001 principles.

inputs.label: Cutting Speed (Vc)
inputs.businessContext: ISO 3685 standard for tool life; affects surface finish and cycle time.

inputs.label: Feed Rate (f)
inputs.businessContext: ISO 3002/1 standard; influences chip load and tool wear.

inputs.label: Depth of Cut (ap)
inputs.businessContext: ISO 3002/1; affects cutting forces and power consumption.

inputs.label: Part Length (L)
inputs.businessContext: Workpiece geometry; defines total travel distance.

inputs.label: Part Diameter (D)
inputs.businessContext: Workpiece diameter; used for rotational speed calculation.

inputs.label: Tool Change Time (Tct)
inputs.businessContext: SMED (Single Minute Exchange of Die) metric; Lean manufacturing.

inputs.label: Tool Life (T)
inputs.businessContext: ISO 3685 Taylor tool life equation; affects cost and downtime.

inputs.label: Number of Passes (n_passes)
inputs.businessContext: Roughing/finishing strategy; impacts total cycle time.

inputs.label: Machine Type
inputs.businessContext: ISO 841 for coordinate systems; affects spindle power and rigidity.

inputs.label: High Feed Strategy
inputs.businessContext: Lean Six Sigma approach to reduce cycle time; may increase tool wear.

rules.message: Feed rate too high relative to cutting speed; risk of poor surface finish (ISO 1302).
Depth of cut exceeds 50% of part diameter; risk of chatter and tool breakage.
Tool life less than 10 minutes; excessive downtime and cost (Lean waste).

thresholds.message: Cycle time exceeds 15 minutes; consider high-feed strategy or multi-tooling.
Estimated cost per part exceeds $50; review tooling and parameters.

formulas.label: Spindle Speed (N)
formulas.description: ISO 841 standard; rotational speed based on cutting speed and diameter.

formulas.label: Cutting Time per Pass (Tc)
formulas.description: Time for one pass; assumes continuous feed.

formulas.label: Total Cutting Time (Tctotal)
formulas.description: Sum of all passes; primary driver of cycle time.

formulas.label: Tool Change Frequency (Ftc)
formulas.description: Number of tool changes required; affects downtime.

formulas.label: Tool Change Downtime (Td)
formulas.description: Total downtime due to tool changes.

formulas.label: High Feed Adjustment (HFA)
formulas.description: Lean improvement factor; reduces cycle time by 15% when active.

formulas.label: Optimized Cycle Time (Topt)
formulas.description: Primary output; total time per part including cutting and tool changes.

formulas.label: Cutting Time (Tctotal)
formulas.description: Alias for total cutting time used by breakdown output.

formulas.label: Optimized Cycle Time (Topt)
formulas.description: Alias for primary cycle time output.

primary.label: Optimized Cycle Time
primary.description: Total time per part after optimization.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Excessive Tool Changes
hiddenLossDrivers.description: High tool change frequency increases downtime; consider longer-life tools or coatings.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Low Spindle Utilization
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Spindle speed below 60% of max; idle time waste (Lean muda).

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Non-Optimal Feed Rate
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Feed rate not aligned with chip thinning; causes increased cycle time.

suggestedActions.label: Increase Feed Rate
suggestedActions.description: If surface finish allows, increase feed by 10-20% to reduce cutting time.

suggestedActions.label: Enable High Feed Strategy
suggestedActions.description: Activate high feed mode to reduce cycle time by 15%.

suggestedActions.label: Optimize Tool Life
suggestedActions.description: Select tool with longer life (e.g., coated carbide) to reduce change frequency.

suggestedActions.label: Reduce Number of Passes
suggestedActions.description: Increase depth of cut if machine power allows; fewer passes reduce total time.
